  1.  Diamond {r3c8, r2c9, r3c9, r5c9} (spine r2c9â€”r3c9).
      â†’ color(r3c8) = color(r5c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r3c8=r5c9], r5c5, r5c6, r6c6} (spine r5c5â€”r5c6).
      â†’ color([r3c8=r5c9]) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c5, [r3c8=r5c9=r6c6], r5c6, r9c6} (spine [r3c8=r5c9=r6c6]â€”r5c6).
      â†’ color(r5c5) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r5c6, [r3c8=r5c9=r6c6], [r5c5=r9c6], r9c8} (spine [r3c8=r5c9=r6c6]â€”[r5c5=r9c6]).
      â†’ color(r5c6) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r3c8=r5c9=r6c6], [r5c5=r9c6], [r5c6=r9c8], r9c2} (spine [r5c5=r9c6]â€”[r5c6=r9c8]).
      â†’ color([r3c8=r5c9=r6c6]) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  6.  Kâ‚„ on {[r3c8=r5c9=r6c6=r9c2], r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. â–¡

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (SET+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {col 3, col 6}â€‰âˆ’â€‰{box 5, box 7}.
      Remainder: {r6c3, r9c6} = {r5c5, r9c2}.
      â†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  SET: {col 6, col 9}â€‰âˆ’â€‰{box 3, box 5}.
      Remainder: {r5c9, r9c6} = {r3c8, r5c5}.
      â†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c8, r5c5, r9c2, r9c8}:
      Diagonals: {r3c8, r9c2} and {r5c5, r9c8} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c8) = color(r9c2) = X
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c2=X â†’ r9c6, r9c8â‰ X
        box 3 {r2c9, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c8=X â†’ r2c9, r3c9â‰ X
        box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r9c2=X â†’ r7c3, r8c3â‰ X
        r3c8=X â†’ r5c5â‰ X (adjacent)
        col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3, r8c3â‰ X â†’ r6c3=X
        col 9 {r2c9, r3c9, r5c9}: r2c9, r3c9â‰ X â†’ r5c9=X
        r5c9=X â†’ r5c6â‰ X (adjacent)
        r6c3=X â†’ r6c6â‰ X (adjacent)
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r9c6}: all â‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r5c5) = color(r9c8) = X
        row 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c5=X â†’ r5c6, r5c9â‰ X
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c8=X â†’ r9c2, r9c6â‰ X
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r5c6, r9c6â‰ X â†’ r6c6=X
        box 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r6c6}: r5c5=X and r6c6=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. â–¡

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===

  1.  Branch on r6c3, r9c8:
      Case A: color(r6c3) = color(r9c8). Identify.
        2.  Kâ‚„ on {[r6c3=r9c8], r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}. Contradiction.

            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.

              r9c8=X â†’ r3c8â‰ X (adjacent)

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_diamond] (SET+xwing+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {col 3, col 6}â€‰âˆ’â€‰{box 5, box 7}.
      Remainder: {r3c6, r6c3} = {r6c5, r9c2}.
      â†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  Branch on r6c5, r9c9:
      Case A: color(r6c5) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r2c9, r3c8, r9c8, [r6c5=r9c9]}:
            Diagonals: {r2c9, r9c8} and {r3c8, [r6c5=r9c9]} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.

              row 9 {[r6c5=r9c9], r9c2, r9c8}: r9c8=X â†’ [r6c5=r9c9], r9c2â‰ X

              r2c9=X â†’ r3c8â‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r9c9], r6c9}: [r6c5=r9c9], r6c9â‰ X â†’ r6c3=X
              col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r6c3=X â†’ r7c3, r8c3â‰ X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: all â‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c8) = color([r6c5=r9c9]) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r9c9], r6c9}: [r6c5=r9c9]=X â†’ r6c3, r6c9â‰ X
              row 9 {[r6c5=r9c9], r9c2, r9c8}: [r6c5=r9c9]=X â†’ r9c2, r9c8â‰ X
              col 9 {r2c9, [r6c5=r9c9], r6c9}: [r6c5=r9c9]=X â†’ r2c9â‰ X
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c6, [r6c5=r9c9]}: [r6c5=r9c9]=X â†’ r4c6, r5c6â‰ X
              r3c8=X â†’ r3c6â‰ X (adjacent)
              col 6 {r3c6, r4c6, r5c6}: all â‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c5) â‰  color(r9c9). Add edge.
        4.  Guardian: r7c3 guards oddagon {r7c3, r6c3, r6c9, r9c9, r9c2} (source r6c5).
            â†’ color(r7c3) = color(r6c5). Identify.

            Bivalue oddagon {r6c3, r6c9, r9c9, r9c2, r8c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (diamond+SET) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c8, r2c9, r3c9, r9c9} (spine r2c9â€”r3c9).

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (SET+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 3, row 9}â€‰âˆ’â€‰{box 3, box 7}.
      Remainder: {r3c6, r9c9} = {r2c9, r8c3}.
      â†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  Branch on r3c6, r6c5:
      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r6c5). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r2c9, r6c9, r6c3, r8c3}:
            Diagonals: {r2c9, r6c3} and {r6c9, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r2c9) = color(r6c3) = X
              row 6 {[r3c6=r6c5], r6c3, r6c9}: r6c3=X â†’ [r3c6=r6c5], r6c9â‰ X

              row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c9}: all â‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c6) â‰  color(r6c5). Add edge.

              box 7 {r8c3, r9c2, r9c3}: r8c3=X â†’ r9c2â‰ X
              r8c3=X â†’ r8c9â‰ X (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c3, r9c6}: all â‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r2c9) â‰  color(r3c3). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r2c9, r3c3, r8c3, r8c9}:
            Diagonals: {r2c9, r8c3} and {r3c3, r8c9} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r2c9) = color(r8c3) = X
              col 3 {r3c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3=X â†’ r3c3, r9c3â‰ X
              col 9 {r2c9, r6c9, r8c9}: r2c9=X â†’ r6c9, r8c9â‰ X
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c2, r9c3}: r8c3=X â†’ r9c2â‰ X
              r2c9=X â†’ r3c8â‰ X (adjacent)
              r8c3=X â†’ r5c6â‰ X (adjacent)
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c8}: r3c3, r3c8â‰ X â†’ r3c6=X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c3, r9c6}: r9c2, r9c3â‰ X â†’ r9c6=X
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r9c6}: r3c6=X and r9c6=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c3) = color(r8c9) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c8}: r3c3=X â†’ r3c6, r3c8â‰ X
              col 3 {r3c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r3c3=X â†’ r8c3, r9c3â‰ X
              col 9 {r2c9, r6c9, r8c9}: r8c9=X â†’ r2c9, r6c9â‰ X
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c2, r9c3}: r8c3, r9c3â‰ X â†’ r9c2=X
              r9c2=X â†’ r6c2, r9c6â‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c9}: r6c2, r6c9â‰ X â†’ r6c5=X
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r9c6}: r3c6, r9c6â‰ X â†’ r5c6=X
              r5c6=X and r6c5=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. â–¡

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Guardian: r3c6 guards oddagon {r3c6, r3c3, r8c3, r9c2, r9c6} (source r9c3).
      â†’ color(r3c6) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c8, r3c3, [r3c6=r9c3], r8c3} (spine r3c3â€”[r3c6=r9c3]).
      â†’ color(r3c8) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r3c8=r8c3], r2c9, r8c9, r6c9} (spine r2c9â€”r8c9).
      â†’ color([r3c8=r8c3]) = color(r6c9). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r5c6, [r3c6=r9c3], r9c6, r9c2} (spine [r3c6=r9c3]â€”r9c6).
      â†’ color(r5c6) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  5.  Kâ‚„ on {[r3c8=r6c9=r8c3], [r5c6=r9c2], r6c2, r6c5}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. â–¡
